The method combines a gradient-updated reconstruction pathway with a history-only predictor, enhancing its ability to detect anomalies that traditional residual-based detectors might miss. AxonAD has demonstrated superior performance on both proprietary automotive telemetry data and benchmark datasets, showcasing its effectiveness in capturing complex dependency shifts in time series data.

zh.title: AxonAD：捕捉时间序列中的结构依赖变化
zh.desc: AxonAD是一种无监督的多变量时间序列异常检测方法，能够通过分析多头注意力机制中的查询演变来识别结构依赖的变化。与传统方法相比，AxonAD在汽车遥测和基准数据集上表现更优。该方法将多头注意力的查询演变视为一个短期可预测的过程，并结合了重建路径和历史预测器。通过这种双重方法，AxonAD能够敏感地捕捉到结构依赖的变化，同时保持对幅度级别异常的检测能力。

papers.title: NanoVDR: Distilling a 2B Vision-Language Retriever into a 70M Text-Only Encoder for Visual Document Retrieval
papers.abstract: Abstract NanoVDR addresses visual document retrieval efficiency by decoupling document indexing from query encoding through a distillation approach that maintains high performance with significantly reduced computational requirements.  					AI-generated summary Vision-Language Model (VLM) based retrievers have advanced visual document retrieval (VDR) to impressive quality. They require the same multi-billion parameter encoder for both document indexing and query encoding, incurring high latency and GPU dependence even for plain-text queries. We observe that this design is unnecessarily symmetric: documents are visually complex and demand strong visual understanding, whereas queries are just short text strings. NanoVDR exploits this query--document asymmetry by decoupling the two encoding paths: a frozen 2B VLM teacher indexes documents offline, while a distilled text-only student as small as 69M parameters encodes queries at inference. The key design choice is the distillation objective. Through systematic comparison of six objectives across three backbones and 22 ViDoRe benchmark datasets, we find that pointwise cosine alignment on query text consistently outperforms ranking-based and contrastive alternatives, while requiring only pre-cached teacher query embeddings and no document processing during training. Furthermore, we identify cross-lingual transfer as the primary performance bottleneck, and resolve it cheaply by augmenting training data with machine-translated queries. The resulting NanoVDR-S-Multi (DistilBERT, 69M) retains 95.1\% of teacher quality and outperforms DSE-Qwen2 (2B) on v2 and v3 with 32times fewer parameters and 50times lower CPU query latency, at a total training cost under 13 GPU-hours.

ru.title: Асимметричная дистилляция для эффективного поиска визуальных документов
ru.desc: NanoVDR решает проблему эффективности поиска визуальных документов, разделяя процессы индексирования документов и кодирования запросов через дистилляцию знаний. Система использует асимметричный подход: большую предобученную Vision-Language Model (VLM) с 2 миллиардами параметров для офлайн-индексирования визуально сложных документов, а для кодирования текстовых запросов применяет компактную дистиллированную модель размером всего 69 миллионов параметров. Авторы выявили, что оптимальной целевой функцией для дистилляции является поточечное выравнивание косинусного сходства на текстах запросов, что превосходит ранжирующие и контрастивные подходы. Использование машинного перевода для аугментации обучающих данных решает проблему кросс-лингвального трансфера, позволяя модели сохранять 95.1% качества учителя при 32-кратном снижении числа параметров и 50-кратном сокращении латентности.

en.title: Decoupling for Efficient Visual Document Retrieval
en.desc: NanoVDR improves visual document retrieval (VDR) by separating the processes of document indexing and query encoding. It uses a large Vision-Language Model (VLM) to index documents while employing a smaller distilled model for encoding queries, which reduces computational load. The study shows that using pointwise cosine alignment for query text leads to better performance compared to other methods. Additionally, it addresses cross-lingual transfer issues by enhancing training data with machine-translated queries, achieving high efficiency with significantly fewer parameters and lower latency.

zh.title: 高效视觉文档检索的新方法
zh.desc: NanoVDR通过将文档索引与查询编码解耦，提升了视觉文档检索的效率。它采用了一种蒸馏方法，使得在保持高性能的同时，显著降低了计算需求。该方法利用了查询和文档之间的非对称性，使用一个大型的视觉语言模型（VLM）教师来离线索引文档，而一个小型的文本蒸馏学生在推理时编码查询。最终，NanoVDR-S-Multi在参数量大幅减少的情况下，仍然保持了接近教师模型的质量，显著降低了查询延迟。

papers.title: Detecting Intrinsic and Instrumental Self-Preservation in Autonomous Agents: The Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel detection framework called UCIP uses quantum statistical mechanics-inspired methods to distinguish between autonomous agents with genuine continuation objectives versus those pursuing continuation only instrumentally, achieving high accuracy in synthetic environments.  					AI-generated summary Autonomous agents, especially delegated systems with memory, persistent context, and multi-step planning, pose a measurement problem not present in stateless models: an agent that preserves continued operation as a terminal objective and one that does so merely instrumentally can produce observationally similar trajectories. External behavioral monitoring cannot reliably distinguish between them. We introduce the Unified Continuation-Interest Protocol (UCIP), a multi-criterion detection framework that moves this distinction from behavior to the latent structure of agent trajectories. UCIP encodes trajectories with a Quantum Boltzmann Machine (QBM), a classical algorithm based on the density-matrix formalism of quantum statistical mechanics, and measures the von Neumann entropy of the reduced density matrix induced by a bipartition of hidden units.   We test whether agents with terminal continuation objectives (Type A) produce latent states with higher entanglement entropy than agents whose continuation is merely instrumental (Type B). Higher entanglement reflects stronger cross-partition statistical coupling.   On gridworld agents with known ground-truth objectives, UCIP achieves 100% detection accuracy and 1.0 AUC-ROC on held-out non-adversarial evaluation under the frozen Phase I gate. The entanglement gap between Type A and Type B agents is Delta = 0.381 (p < 0.001, permutation test). Pearson r = 0.934 across an 11-point interpolation sweep indicates that, within this synthetic family, UCIP tracks graded changes in continuation weighting rather than merely a binary label. Among the tested models, only the QBM achieves positive Delta. All computations are classical; "quantum" refers only to the mathematical formalism. UCIP does not detect consciousness or subjective experience; it detects statistical structure in latent representations that correlates with known objectives.

ru.title: Квантовая статистика для диагностики истинных целей в поведении агентов
ru.desc: В статье предложена инновационная система UCIP для различения автономных агентов, которые рассматривают своё продолжение работы как конечную цель, от агентов, для которых это лишь инструмент. Метод использует квантовую машину Больцмана (QBM) для кодирования траекторий поведения агентов и анализирует энтропию фон Неймана редуцированной матрицы плотности. Агенты с истинной целью продолжения показывают более высокую энтангл-энтропию, отражающую сильную статистическую связь между разделами скрытого представления. На синтетических средах система достигает 100% точности обнаружения и коррелирует с градуальными изменениями в весах целей агентов (Pearson r = 0.934).

en.title: Distinguishing Genuine Goals from Instrumental Actions in Autonomous Agents
en.desc: The paper presents a new detection framework called UCIP, which uses concepts from quantum statistical mechanics to differentiate between autonomous agents with genuine continuation objectives and those that pursue continuation only as a means to an end. It addresses the challenge of distinguishing between these types of agents, which can produce similar observable behaviors, by analyzing the latent structure of their trajectories instead. UCIP employs a Quantum Boltzmann Machine to encode these trajectories and measures the von Neumann entropy to assess the entanglement of the agents' latent states. The framework demonstrates high accuracy in identifying the agents' objectives, achieving 100% detection accuracy in tests with synthetic gridworld agents.

zh.title: UCIP：区分真实与工具目标的智能代理
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的检测框架UCIP，旨在区分具有真实持续目标的自主代理与仅仅将持续作为工具的代理。UCIP利用量子统计力学的原理，通过量子玻尔兹曼机对代理的轨迹进行编码，并测量其隐含单元的约简密度矩阵的冯·诺依曼熵。研究表明，具有终极持续目标的代理（A类）在潜在状态中表现出更高的纠缠熵，与仅作为工具的代理（B类）相比，反映出更强的统计耦合。UCIP在已知目标的网格世界代理中实现了100%的检测准确率，表明其在区分代理目标方面的有效性。

papers.title: ECoLAD: Deployment-Oriented Evaluation for Automotive Time-Series Anomaly Detection
papers.abstract: Abstract ECoLAD presents a deployment-focused evaluation framework for time-series anomaly detection that assesses performance under compute constraints using a systematic ladder of efficiency reductions.  					AI-generated summary Time-series anomaly detectors are commonly compared on workstation-class hardware under unconstrained execution. In-vehicle monitoring, however, requires predictable latency and stable behavior under limited CPU parallelism. Accuracy-only leaderboards can therefore misrepresent which methods remain feasible under deployment-relevant constraints.   We present ECoLAD (Efficiency Compute Ladder for Anomaly Detection), a deployment-oriented evaluation protocol instantiated as an empirical study on proprietary automotive telemetry (anomaly rate {approx}0.022) and complementary public benchmarks. ECoLAD applies a monotone compute-reduction ladder across heterogeneous detector families using mechanically determined, integer-only scaling rules and explicit CPU thread caps, while logging every applied configuration change. Throughput-constrained behavior is characterized by sweeping target scoring rates and reporting (i) coverage (the fraction of entities meeting the target) and (ii) the best AUC-PR achievable among measured ladder configurations satisfying the target. On constrained automotive telemetry, lightweight classical detectors sustain both coverage and detection lift above the random baseline across the full throughput sweep. Several deep methods lose feasibility before they lose accuracy.
affiliations: Institute for Intelligent Systems, Esslingen University of Applied Sciences, Germany

ru.title: Оценка детекторов аномалий в реальных условиях ограниченных ресурсов
ru.desc: ECoLAD — это фреймворк для оценки моделей обнаружения аномалий во временных рядах, который фокусируется на реальных условиях развертывания с ограничениями вычислительных ресурсов. Авторы предлагают систематическую методологию, которая постепенно снижает доступные вычисления и измеряет, как детекторы аномалий справляются при ограниченной пропускной способности CPU. Исследование проводится на реальной автомобильной телеметрии и публичных бенчмарках, с явным контролем параллелизма потоков и логированием всех изменений конфигурации. Результаты показывают, что классические легкие модели сохраняют эффективность под ограничениями, в то время как некоторые глубокие методы машинного обучения теряют практическую применимость раньше, чем теряют точность.

en.title: ECoLAD: Evaluating Anomaly Detection Under Real-World Constraints
en.desc: ECoLAD introduces a new framework for evaluating time-series anomaly detection methods, focusing on their performance under limited computational resources. This framework systematically reduces efficiency to assess how well different detection algorithms perform when faced with real-world constraints, such as those found in automotive applications. The study highlights that traditional accuracy metrics can be misleading, as some deep learning methods may become impractical before they show a drop in accuracy. By using a structured approach to measure coverage and detection performance, ECoLAD provides insights into which algorithms are most suitable for deployment in resource-constrained environments.

zh.title: ECoLAD：优化部署的异常检测评估框架
zh.desc: ECoLAD是一个专注于部署的时间序列异常检测评估框架，旨在在计算约束下评估性能。该框架通过系统化的效率降低阶梯，分析不同检测器在有限CPU并行性下的表现。传统的准确性排行榜可能无法真实反映在实际部署中可行的方法。ECoLAD通过对汽车遥测数据进行实证研究，展示了在不同配置下的检测覆盖率和最佳AUC-PR。

papers.title: Fine-grained Motion Retrieval via Joint-Angle Motion Images and Token-Patch Late Interaction
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel text-motion retrieval approach uses joint-angle-based motion representation and Vision Transformer-compatible pseudo-images to achieve superior performance with interpretable fine-grained alignments.  					AI-generated summary Text-motion retrieval aims to learn a semantically aligned latent space between natural language descriptions and 3D human motion skeleton sequences, enabling bidirectional search across the two modalities. Most existing methods use a dual-encoder framework that compresses motion and text into global embeddings, discarding fine-grained local correspondences, and thus reducing accuracy. Additionally, these global-embedding methods offer limited interpretability of the retrieval results. To overcome these limitations, we propose an interpretable, joint-angle-based motion representation that maps joint-level local features into a structured pseudo-image, compatible with pre-trained Vision Transformers. For text-to-motion retrieval, we employ MaxSim, a token-wise late interaction mechanism, and enhance it with Masked Language Modeling regularization to foster robust, interpretable text-motion alignment. Extensive experiments on HumanML3D and KIT-ML show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art text-motion retrieval approaches while offering interpretable fine-grained correspondences between text and motion. The code is available in the supplementary material.
affiliations: Georgia Institute of Technology, Atlanta GA, USA

ru.title: Интерпретируемый поиск движений по тексту через структурированные представления суставов
ru.desc: В статье предлагается новый подход для поиска соответствий между текстовыми описаниями и трёхмерными движениями человека. Авторы используют представление движения через углы суставов, преобразуя их в псевдо-изображения, совместимые с предобученными Vision Transformers. Для достижения точного выравнивания текста и движения применяется механизм MaxSim с поэлементным взаимодействием и регуляризацией маскированного языкового моделирования. Экспериментальные результаты показывают превосходство метода над аналогами, при этом обеспечивая интерпретируемость результатов поиска на уровне отдельных элементов.

en.title: Bridging Text and Motion with Fine-Grained Interpretability
en.desc: This paper presents a new method for text-motion retrieval that improves how we connect written descriptions with 3D human movements. It introduces a joint-angle-based representation that captures detailed motion features and transforms them into pseudo-images, which can be processed by Vision Transformers. Unlike previous methods that use global embeddings, this approach maintains fine-grained local correspondences, enhancing both accuracy and interpretability. The results show that this method significantly outperforms existing techniques while providing clearer insights into the relationships between text and motion.

zh.title: 创新的文本-运动检索方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的文本-运动检索方法，利用基于关节角度的运动表示和与视觉变换器兼容的伪图像，实现了更优的性能和可解释的细粒度对齐。该方法通过学习自然语言描述与3D人类运动骨架序列之间的语义对齐潜在空间，支持双向检索。与现有的双编码器框架不同，我们的方法保留了局部特征的细粒度对应关系，从而提高了检索的准确性和可解释性。通过在HumanML3D和KIT-ML数据集上的广泛实验，我们的方法在文本-运动检索任务中超越了现有的最先进技术。
